#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# aos-sweep.sh — Exit scrub for all AOS repos
# Stages dirty trees, commits as WIP, pushes to remote.
# Run this before walking away or switching machines.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

TIMESTAMP=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
SHORT_TS=$(date '+%Y%m%d-%H%M')

# ── Build a contextual commit message from git status --porcelain ────
# Groups files by action and top-level directory, names key files.
# Usage: msg=$(build_commit_msg "$porcelain" "$reponame")
build_commit_msg() {
    local porcelain="$1" repo="$2"
    local -A dirs_modified=() dirs_added=() dirs_deleted=()
    local -a key_files=()

    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        local action="${line:0:2}"
        local fpath="${line:3}"
        fpath="${fpath## }"                     # trim leading space
        fpath="${fpath#\"}" ; fpath="${fpath%\"}" # strip quotes

        # Top-level directory (or filename if at root)
        local tld="${fpath%%/*}"
        [[ "$tld" == "$fpath" ]] && tld="(root)"

        # Classify
        case "$action" in
            '??')       dirs_added["$tld"]=$(( ${dirs_added[$tld]:-0} + 1 )) ;;
            ' D'|'D '|'DD') dirs_deleted["$tld"]=$(( ${dirs_deleted[$tld]:-0} + 1 )) ;;
            *)          dirs_modified["$tld"]=$(( ${dirs_modified[$tld]:-0} + 1 )) ;;
        esac

        # Collect notable filenames (skip noise like .pyc, __pycache__)
        local base="${fpath##*/}"
        case "$base" in
            *.pyc|*.pyo|__pycache__) continue ;;
        esac
        if (( ${#key_files[@]} < 5 )); then
            key_files+=("$base")
        fi
    done <<< "$porcelain"

    # Build summary fragments: "update src/ tests/", "add config/", "remove old/"
    local -a frags=()
    if (( ${#dirs_modified[@]} > 0 )); then
        local names
        names=$(printf '%s\n' "${!dirs_modified[@]}" | sort | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
        frags+=("update ${names% }")
    fi
    if (( ${#dirs_added[@]} > 0 )); then
        local names
        names=$(printf '%s\n' "${!dirs_added[@]}" | sort | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
        frags+=("add ${names% }")
    fi
    if (( ${#dirs_deleted[@]} > 0 )); then
        local names
        names=$(printf '%s\n' "${!dirs_deleted[@]}" | sort | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
        frags+=("remove ${names% }")
    fi

    local summary
    local IFS_OLD="$IFS"
    summary=""
    for ((i=0; i<${#frags[@]}; i++)); do
        (( i > 0 )) && summary+="; "
        summary+="${frags[$i]}"
    done
    IFS="$IFS_OLD"

    # Key files addendum (most interesting changed files)
    local keylist=""
    if (( ${#key_files[@]} > 0 )); then
        keylist=$(printf '%s, ' "${key_files[@]}"); keylist="${keylist%, }"
    fi

    local msg="wip($repo): $summary"
    # Append key files if message isn't already too long
    if [[ -n "$keylist" ]] && (( ${#msg} + ${#keylist} + 3 < 120 )); then
        msg="$msg ($keylist)"
    fi
    echo "$msg"
}

# Every git repo under ~/code
REPOS=()
for d in /home/ilyac/code/*/; do
    [ -d "$d/.git" ] && REPOS+=("$d")
done

swept=()
skipped=()
failed=()

echo ""
echo "================================================================"
echo "  AOS EXIT SWEEP — $TIMESTAMP"
echo "================================================================"
echo ""

for repo in "${REPOS[@]}"; do
    name=$(basename "$repo")
    cd "$repo"

    # Check if dirty (untracked + modified + staged)
    if git diff --quiet HEAD 2>/dev/null && [ -z "$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
        skipped+=("$name")
        printf "  %-20s  %s\n" "$name" "clean — skipped"
        continue
    fi

    branch=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null)

    # ── Describe changes ──────────────────────────────────────────
    echo ""
    echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
    echo "  REPO: $name  (branch: $branch)"
    echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"

    porcelain=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)

    # Categorise changes
    new_files=$(echo "$porcelain" | grep -c '^??' || true)
    modified_files=$(echo "$porcelain" | grep -c '^ M\|^MM\|^ T' || true)
    deleted_files=$(echo "$porcelain" | grep -c '^ D' || true)
    staged_files=$(echo "$porcelain" | grep -c '^[MARCD] ' || true)
    total_files=$(echo "$porcelain" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')

    echo ""
    echo "  Changes ($total_files files):"
    [ "$new_files" -gt 0 ]      && echo "    + $new_files new (untracked)"
    [ "$modified_files" -gt 0 ]  && echo "    ~ $modified_files modified"
    [ "$deleted_files" -gt 0 ]   && echo "    - $deleted_files deleted"
    [ "$staged_files" -gt 0 ]    && echo "    * $staged_files already staged"
    echo ""
    echo "  Files:"
    echo "$porcelain" | head -20 | sed 's/^/    /'
    [ "$total_files" -gt 20 ] && echo "    ... and $((total_files - 20)) more"

    # Build a contextual commit message from actual file paths
    msg=$(build_commit_msg "$porcelain" "$name")

    echo ""
    echo "  Commit message: $msg"
    echo ""

    # ── Ask permission ────────────────────────────────────────────
    read -rp "  Commit and push $name? [y/N] " answer </dev/tty
    if [[ ! "$answer" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
        skipped+=("$name (declined)")
        printf "  %-20s  %s\n" "$name" "skipped (user declined)"
        continue
    fi

    # Stage everything
    git add -A

    # Commit
    git commit -m "$msg" --no-verify >/dev/null 2>&1

    # Push
    if git push origin "$branch" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        swept+=("$name ($branch)")
        printf "  %-20s  %s\n" "$name" "swept + pushed ($branch)"
    else
        failed+=("$name ($branch)")
        printf "  %-20s  %s\n" "$name" "!! PUSH FAILED !!"
    fi
done

# ── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "  SUMMARY"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo ""
echo "  Repos scanned:  ${#REPOS[@]}"
echo "  Clean/skipped:  ${#skipped[@]}"
echo "  Swept + pushed: ${#swept[@]}"

if [ ${#swept[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    for s in "${swept[@]}"; do
        echo "    -> $s"
    done
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# aos-sweep.sh — Exit scrub for all AOS repos
# Stages dirty trees, commits as WIP, pushes to remote.
# Run this before walking away or switching machines.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

TIMESTAMP=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
SHORT_TS=$(date '+%Y%m%d-%H%M')

# ── Build a contextual commit message from git status --porcelain ────
# Groups files by action and top-level directory, names key files.
# Usage: msg=$(build_commit_msg "$porcelain" "$reponame")
build_commit_msg() {
    local porcelain="$1" repo="$2"
    local -A dirs_modified=() dirs_added=() dirs_deleted=()
    local -a key_files=()

    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        local action="${line:0:2}"
        local fpath="${line:3}"
        fpath="${fpath## }"                     # trim leading space
        fpath="${fpath#\"}" ; fpath="${fpath%\"}" # strip quotes

        # Top-level directory (or filename if at root)
        local tld="${fpath%%/*}"
        [[ "$tld" == "$fpath" ]] && tld="(root)"

        # Classify
        case "$action" in
            '??')       dirs_added["$tld"]=$(( ${dirs_added[$tld]:-0} + 1 )) ;;
            ' D'|'D '|'DD') dirs_deleted["$tld"]=$(( ${dirs_deleted[$tld]:-0} + 1 )) ;;
            *)          dirs_modified["$tld"]=$(( ${dirs_modified[$tld]:-0} + 1 )) ;;
        esac

        # Collect notable filenames (skip noise like .pyc, __pycache__)
        local base="${fpath##*/}"
        case "$base" in
            *.pyc|*.pyo|__pycache__) continue ;;
        esac
        if (( ${#key_files[@]} < 5 )); then
            key_files+=("$base")
        fi
    done <<< "$porcelain"

    # Build summary fragments: "update src/ tests/", "add config/", "remove old/"
    local -a frags=()
    if (( ${#dirs_modified[@]} > 0 )); then
        local names
        names=$(printf '%s\n' "${!dirs_modified[@]}" | sort | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
        frags+=("update ${names% }")
    fi
    if (( ${#dirs_added[@]} > 0 )); then
        local names
        names=$(printf '%s\n' "${!dirs_added[@]}" | sort | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
        frags+=("add ${names% }")
    fi
    if (( ${#dirs_deleted[@]} > 0 )); then
        local names
        names=$(printf '%s\n' "${!dirs_deleted[@]}" | sort | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
        frags+=("remove ${names% }")
    fi

    local summary
    local IFS_OLD="$IFS"
    summary=""
    for ((i=0; i<${#frags[@]}; i++)); do
        (( i > 0 )) && summary+="; "
        summary+="${frags[$i]}"
    done
    IFS="$IFS_OLD"

    # Key files addendum (most interesting changed files)
    local keylist=""
    if (( ${#key_files[@]} > 0 )); then
        keylist=$(printf '%s, ' "${key_files[@]}"); keylist="${keylist%, }"
    fi

    local msg="wip($repo): $summary"
    # Append key files if message isn't already too long
    if [[ -n "$keylist" ]] && (( ${#msg} + ${#keylist} + 3 < 120 )); then
        msg="$msg ($keylist)"
    fi
    echo "$msg"
}

# Every git repo under ~/code
REPOS=()
for d in /home/ilyac/code/*/; do
    [ -d "$d/.git" ] && REPOS+=("$d")
done

swept=()
skipped=()
failed=()

echo ""
echo "================================================================"
echo "  AOS EXIT SWEEP — $TIMESTAMP"
echo "================================================================"
echo ""

for repo in "${REPOS[@]}"; do
    name=$(basename "$repo")
    cd "$repo"

    # Check if dirty (untracked + modified + staged)
    if git diff --quiet HEAD 2>/dev/null && [ -z "$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
        skipped+=("$name")
        printf "  %-20s  %s\n" "$name" "clean — skipped"
        continue
    fi

    branch=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null)

    # ── Describe changes ──────────────────────────────────────────
    echo ""
    echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
    echo "  REPO: $name  (branch: $branch)"
    echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"

    porcelain=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)

    # Categorise changes
    new_files=$(echo "$porcelain" | grep -c '^??' || true)
    modified_files=$(echo "$porcelain" | grep -c '^ M\|^MM\|^ T' || true)
    deleted_files=$(echo "$porcelain" | grep -c '^ D' || true)
    staged_files=$(echo "$porcelain" | grep -c '^[MARCD] ' || true)
    total_files=$(echo "$porcelain" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')

    echo ""
    echo "  Changes ($total_files files):"
    [ "$new_files" -gt 0 ]      && echo "    + $new_files new (untracked)"
    [ "$modified_files" -gt 0 ]  && echo "    ~ $modified_files modified"
    [ "$deleted_files" -gt 0 ]   && echo "    - $deleted_files deleted"
    [ "$staged_files" -gt 0 ]    && echo "    * $staged_files already staged"
    echo ""
    echo "  Files:"
    echo "$porcelain" | head -20 | sed 's/^/    /'
    [ "$total_files" -gt 20 ] && echo "    ... and $((total_files - 20)) more"

    # Build a contextual commit message from actual file paths
    msg=$(build_commit_msg "$porcelain" "$name")

    echo ""
    echo "  Commit message: $msg"
    echo ""

    # ── Ask permission ────────────────────────────────────────────
    read -rp "  Commit and push $name? [y/N] " answer </dev/tty
    if [[ ! "$answer" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
        skipped+=("$name (declined)")
        printf "  %-20s  %s\n" "$name" "skipped (user declined)"
        continue
    fi

    # Stage everything
    git add -A

    # Commit
    git commit -m "$msg" --no-verify >/dev/null 2>&1

    # Push
    if git push origin "$branch" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        swept+=("$name ($branch)")
        printf "  %-20s  %s\n" "$name" "swept + pushed ($branch)"
    else
        failed+=("$name ($branch)")
        printf "  %-20s  %s\n" "$name" "!! PUSH FAILED !!"
    fi
done

# ── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "  SUMMARY"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo ""
echo "  Repos scanned:  ${#REPOS[@]}"
echo "  Clean/skipped:  ${#skipped[@]}"
echo "  Swept + pushed: ${#swept[@]}"

if [ ${#swept[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    for s in "${swept[@]}"; do
        echo "    -> $s"
    done
fi

if [ ${#failed[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "================================================================"
    echo "  !! PUSH FAILURES — DO NOT WALK AWAY !!"
    echo "================================================================"
    echo ""
    for f in "${failed[@]}"; do
        echo "    FAILED: $f"
    done
    echo ""
    echo "  These repos have LOCAL COMMITS that are NOT on the remote."
    echo "  Fix the push before switching machines."
    echo "================================================================"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "  All clean. Safe to walk away."
echo ""

# ── Ask permission ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    local answer
    read -rp "  Commit and push $name? [y/N] " answer </dev/tty
    if [[ ! "$answer" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
      clean+=("$name (declined)")
      info "$name: skipped (user declined)"
      continue
    fi

    # Dirty — sweep it
    (
      cd "$cwd"
      if [[ "$branch" == "main" ]]; then
        git checkout -b "wip/auto-sweep-${ts}" 2>&1 | sed "s/^/  /"
      fi
      git add -A
      git commit -m "$commit_msg" 2>&1 | sed "s/^/  /"
      git push -u origin HEAD 2>&1 | sed "s/^/  /"
    ) || { warn "$name: sweep failed (continuing)"; continue; }

    local sweep_branch
    sweep_branch=$(cd "$cwd" && git branch --show-current)
    ok "$name: swept on ${sweep_branch} (was dirty on ${branch})"
    swept+=("$name:${sweep_branch}")
  done

  # ── Step 2: Pull ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  header "Pulling latest code..."
  for svc in "${_chosen[@]}"; do
    entry=$(get_service "$svc")
    IFS='|' read -r name cwd _ _ _ _ <<< "$entry"
    [[ ! -d "$cwd/.git" ]] && continue

    info "git pull $name"
    local pull_output
    if pull_output=$(cd "$cwd" && git pull --ff-only 2>&1); then
      echo "$pull_output" | sed "s/^/  /"
      if echo "$pull_output" | grep -q "Already up to date"; then
        : # no new changes

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# ─── AOS Service Launcher ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# Multi-select menu → auto-sweep → git pull → pm2 backends → Vite frontends → browser → Claude CLI
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

BASE="$HOME/code"
PM2="npx pm2"
CLAUDE_MODEL="opus"

# ── Preamble — loads .env + activates .venv before each service command ──
ENV='set -a && source .env 2>/dev/null && set +a;'
VENV="${ENV} source .venv/bin/activate 2>/dev/null;"

# ── Service definitions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Format: NAME|CWD|BACKEND_CMD|BACKEND_PORT|FRONTEND_DIR|FRONTEND_PORT
#   FRONTEND_DIR: subdir relative to CWD (empty = no separate frontend)
#   FRONTEND_PORT: explicit port to pass to Vite (avoids collisions)
# Ports verified via testing on 2026-02-26:
#   Backends: aod=8001 aam=8002 farm=8003 dcl=8004 nlq=8005 platform=8006 revops=8007 finops=5000 onboard=3000
#   Frontends: dcl=3004 nlq=3005 platform=3006 revops=3007 onboard=3008
#   Note: AOD frontend (client/) is just a marketing component embedded via iframe;
#         the actual AOD console UI is served by the backend at :8001
declare -a SERVICES=(
  "aod|$BASE/aod|${VENV} PYTHONPATH=./src python3 -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8001 --reload|8001||"
  "aam|$BASE/aam|${VENV} python3 -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8002 --reload|8002||"
  "dcl|$BASE/dcl|${VENV} python3 -m uvicorn backend.api.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8004 --reload|8004|.|3004"
  "farm|$BASE/farm|${VENV} python3 -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8003|8003||"
  "nlq|$BASE/nlq|${VENV} python3 -m uvicorn src.nlq.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8005 --reload|8005|.|3005"
  "platform|$BASE/platform|${VENV} PORT=8006 bash start.sh|8006|frontend|3006"
  "finops|$BASE/finops|${ENV} npx tsx server/index.ts|5000||"
  "revops|$BASE/revops|${VENV} python3 -m uvicorn api:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8007 --reload|8007|frontend|3007"
  "onboard|$BASE/dcl-onboarding-agent|${ENV} npx tsx watch src/server/index.ts|3000|.|3008"
  "site|$BASE/aos_site|${ENV} npx vite --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5173|5173||"
  "console|$BASE/console|${VENV} python3 -m uvicorn backend.app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8009 --reload|8009|frontend|3009"
  "convergence|$BASE/convergence|${VENV} python3 -m uvicorn backend.api.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8010 --reload|8010|.|3010"
)

ALL_NAMES=()
for entry in "${SERVICES[@]}"; do
  IFS='|' read -r name _ _ _ _ _ <<< "$entry"
  ALL_NAMES+=("$name")
done

# ── Preset groups ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
declare -A PRESETS=(
  ["core"]="aod aam farm dcl nlq platform console convergence"
)

# ── Color helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
RED='\033[0;31m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
NC='\033[0m'

info()  { echo -e "${CYAN}▸${NC} $*"; }
ok()    { echo -e "${GREEN}✔${NC} $*"; }
warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠${NC} $*"; }
err()   { echo -e "${RED}✖${NC} $*"; }
header(){ echo -e "\n${BOLD}$*${NC}"; }

# ── Build a contextual commit message from git status --porcelain ────
# Groups files by action and top-level directory, names key files.
build_commit_msg() {
    local porcelain="$1" repo="$2"
    local -A dirs_modified=() dirs_added=() dirs_deleted=()
    local -a key_files=()

    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        local action="${line:0:2}"
        local fpath="${line:3}"
        fpath="${fpath## }"
        fpath="${fpath#\"}" ; fpath="${fpath%\"}"

        local tld="${fpath%%/*}"
        [[ "$tld" == "$fpath" ]] && tld="(root)"

        case "$action" in
            '??')       dirs_added["$tld"]=$(( ${dirs_added[$tld]:-0} + 1 )) ;;
            ' D'|'D '|'DD') dirs_deleted["$tld"]=$(( ${dirs_deleted[$tld]:-0} + 1 )) ;;
            *)          dirs_modified["$tld"]=$(( ${dirs_modified[$tld]:-0} + 1 )) ;;
        esac

        local base="${fpath##*/}"
        case "$base" in
            *.pyc|*.pyo|__pycache__) continue ;;
        esac
        if (( ${#key_files[@]} < 5 )); then
            key_files+=("$base")
        fi
    done <<< "$porcelain"

    local -a frags=()
    if (( ${#dirs_modified[@]} > 0 )); then
        local names
        names=$(printf '%s\n' "${!dirs_modified[@]}" | sort | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
        frags+=("update ${names% }")
    fi
    if (( ${#dirs_added[@]} > 0 )); then
        local names
        names=$(printf '%s\n' "${!dirs_added[@]}" | sort | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
        frags+=("add ${names% }")
    fi
    if (( ${#dirs_deleted[@]} > 0 )); then
        local names
        names=$(printf '%s\n' "${!dirs_deleted[@]}" | sort | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
        frags+=("remove ${names% }")
    fi

    local summary
    summary=""
    for ((i=0; i<${#frags[@]}; i++)); do
        (( i > 0 )) && summary+="; "
        summary+="${frags[$i]}"
    done

    local keylist=""
    if (( ${#key_files[@]} > 0 )); then
        keylist=$(printf '%s, ' "${key_files[@]}"); keylist="${keylist%, }"
    fi

    local msg="wip($repo): $summary"
    if [[ -n "$keylist" ]] && (( ${#msg} + ${#keylist} + 3 < 120 )); then
        msg="$msg ($keylist)"
    fi
    echo "$msg"
}

# ── Browser opener — WSL-aware ──────────────────────────────────────────
open_url() {
  local url="$1"
  if grep -qi microsoft /proc/version 2>/dev/null; then
    powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command "Start-Process '$url'" &>/dev/null
    sleep 0.3
  elif command -v xdg-open &>/dev/null; then
    xdg-open "$url" 2>/dev/null
  elif command -v open &>/dev/null; then
    open "$url" 2>/dev/null
  else
    warn "No browser opener found — open manually: $url"
    return 1
  fi
}

# ── Multi-select menu ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
select_services() {
  # Build preset labels for display
  local -a preset_labels=()
  for key in "${!PRESETS[@]}"; do
    preset_labels+=("launch $key  [${PRESETS[$key]}]")
  done

  if command -v fzf &>/dev/null; then
    local pick
    pick=$(printf '%s\n' "${preset_labels[@]}" "${ALL_NAMES[@]}" \
      | fzf --multi --prompt="Select services (TAB to toggle, ENTER to confirm): " \
             --header="AOS Service Launcher" \
             --height=$((${#ALL_NAMES[@]} + ${#preset_labels[@]} + 4)) --reverse --border </dev/tty)

    # Expand any selected presets into individual service names
    while IFS= read -r line; do
      local matched=0
      for key in "${!PRESETS[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$line" == "launch $key"* ]]; then
          printf '%s\n' ${PRESETS[$key]}
          matched=1
          break
        fi
      done
      (( matched )) || echo "$line"
    done <<< "$pick" | sort -u
  else
    echo "" >&2
    header "AOS Service Launcher" >&2
    echo "Enter numbers separated by spaces (e.g. 1 3 5), 'a' for all, or 'c' for core:" >&2
    echo "" >&2
    printf "  ${YELLOW}c${NC}) launch core  [${PRESETS[core]}]\n" >&2
    echo "" >&2
    for i in "${!ALL_NAMES[@]}"; do
      printf "  ${CYAN}%d${NC}) %s\n" $((i+1)) "${ALL_NAMES[$i]}" >&2
    done
    echo "" >&2
    read -rp "Selection: " input </dev/tty

    if [[ "$input" == "a" || "$input" == "all" ]]; then
      printf '%s\n' "${ALL_NAMES[@]}"
      return
    fi

    if [[ "$input" == "c" || "$input" == "core" ]]; then
      printf '%s\n' ${PRESETS[core]}
      return
    fi

    for num in $input; do
      idx=$((num - 1))
      if (( idx >= 0 && idx < ${#ALL_NAMES[@]} )); then
        echo "${ALL_NAMES[$idx]}"
      else
        warn "Invalid selection: $num (skipped)" >&2
      fi
    done
  fi
}

# ── Lookup service entry by name ────────────────────────────────────────
get_service() {
  local target="$1"
  for entry in "${SERVICES[@]}"; do
    IFS='|' read -r name _ _ _ _ _ <<< "$entry"
    if [[ "$name" == "$target" ]]; then
      echo "$entry"
      return
    fi
  done
}

# ── Kill any process occupying a port ──────────────────────────────────
free_port() {
  local port="$1"
  local pids
  pids=$(ss -tlnp "sport = :$port" 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR>1 {match($0, /pid=([0-9]+)/, m); if(m[1]) print m[1]}' | sort -u)
  if [[ -n "$pids" ]]; then
    for pid in $pids; do
      warn "Killing stale process $pid on port $port"
      kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    done
    sleep 0.5
    for pid in $pids; do
      if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
      fi
    done
  fi
}

# ── Stop all services ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
stop_all() {
  header "Stopping all AOS services..."
  $PM2 delete all 2>/dev/null && ok "pm2 processes stopped" || info "No pm2 processes running"
  pkill -f "npm run dev" 2>/dev/null || true
  pkill -f "npx vite" 2>/dev/null || true
  ok "All AOS services stopped."
  exit 0
}

# ── Global gitignore — one-time WSL hygiene ───────────────────────────
setup_global_gitignore() {
  local ggi="$HOME/.gitignore_global"
  if ! grep -qF '*:Zone.Identifier' "$ggi" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo '# WSL Zone.Identifier metadata files' >> "$ggi"
    echo '*:Zone.Identifier' >> "$ggi"
    git config --global core.excludesfile "$ggi"
    ok "Added *:Zone.Identifier to global gitignore"
  fi
}

# ── Auto-sweep + pull ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Step 1: Sweep dirty repos (stage, commit, push WIP)
# Step 2: Pull latest from remote
# Prints per-repo status and a summary at the end.
sweep_and_pull() {
  local -n _chosen=$1
  local ts
  ts=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
  local -a swept=() clean=() pulled=() pull_failed=()

  # ── Step 1: Auto-sweep ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  header "Auto-sweep: saving any dirty working trees..."
  for svc in "${_chosen[@]}"; do
    entry=$(get_service "$svc")
    IFS='|' read -r name cwd _ _ _ _ <<< "$entry"
    [[ ! -d "$cwd/.git" ]] && continue

    local status branch
    status=$(cd "$cwd" && git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)
    branch=$(cd "$cwd" && git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)

    if [[ -z "$status" ]]; then
      ok "$name: clean"
      clean+=("$name")
      continue
    fi

    # ── Describe changes ────────────────────────────────────────────
    echo ""
    echo "  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
    echo "  REPO: $name  (branch: $branch)"
    echo "  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"

    local new_files modified_files deleted_files staged_files total_files
    new_files=$(echo "$status" | grep -c '^??' || true)
    modified_files=$(echo "$status" | grep -c '^ M\|^MM\|^ T' || true)
    deleted_files=$(echo "$status" | grep -c '^ D' || true)
    staged_files=$(echo "$status" | grep -c '^[MARCD] ' || true)
    total_files=$(echo "$status" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')

    echo ""
    echo "  Changes ($total_files files):"
    [ "$new_files" -gt 0 ]      && echo "    + $new_files new (untracked)"
    [ "$modified_files" -gt 0 ]  && echo "    ~ $modified_files modified"
    [ "$deleted_files" -gt 0 ]   && echo "    - $deleted_files deleted"
    [ "$staged_files" -gt 0 ]    && echo "    * $staged_files already staged"
    echo ""
    echo "  Files:"
    echo "$status" | head -20 | sed 's/^/    /'
    [ "$total_files" -gt 20 ] && echo "    ... and $((total_files - 20)) more"

    # Build a contextual commit message from actual file paths
    local commit_msg
    commit_msg=$(build_commit_msg "$status" "$name")

    echo ""
    echo "  Commit message: $commit_msg"
    echo ""

    # ── Ask permission ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    local answer
    read -rp "  Commit and push $name? [y/N] " answer </dev/tty
    if [[ ! "$answer" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
      clean+=("$name (declined)")
      info "$name: skipped (user declined)"
      continue
    fi

    # Dirty — sweep it
    (
      cd "$cwd"
      if [[ "$branch" == "main" ]]; then
        git checkout -b "wip/auto-sweep-${ts}" 2>&1 | sed "s/^/  /"
      fi
      git add -A
      git commit -m "$commit_msg" 2>&1 | sed "s/^/  /"
      git push -u origin HEAD 2>&1 | sed "s/^/  /"
    ) || { warn "$name: sweep failed (continuing)"; continue; }

    local sweep_branch
    sweep_branch=$(cd "$cwd" && git branch --show-current)
    ok "$name: swept on ${sweep_branch} (was dirty on ${branch})"
    swept+=("$name:${sweep_branch}")
  done

  # ── Step 2: Pull ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  header "Pulling latest code..."
  for svc in "${_chosen[@]}"; do
    entry=$(get_service "$svc")
    IFS='|' read -r name cwd _ _ _ _ <<< "$entry"
    [[ ! -d "$cwd/.git" ]] && continue

    info "git pull $name"
    local pull_output
    if pull_output=$(cd "$cwd" && git pull --ff-only 2>&1); then
      echo "$pull_output" | sed "s/^/  /"
      if echo "$pull_output" | grep -q "Already up to date"; then
        : # no new changes
      else
        pulled+=("$name")
      fi
    else
      echo "$pull_output" | sed "s/^/  /"
      warn "$name: git pull failed (continuing)"
      pull_failed+=("$name")
    fi
  done

  # ── Summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  header "Sync summary"
  echo ""
  if (( ${#swept[@]} > 0 )); then
    for s in "${swept[@]}"; do
      IFS=':' read -r sname sbranch <<< "$s"
      echo -e "  ${YELLOW}swept${NC}   $sname → $sbranch"
    done
  fi
  if (( ${#clean[@]} > 0 )); then
    echo -e "  ${GREEN}clean${NC}   ${clean[*]}"
  fi
  if (( ${#pulled[@]} > 0 )); then
    echo -e "  ${CYAN}pulled${NC}  ${pulled[*]}"
  fi
  if (( ${#pull_failed[@]} > 0 )); then
    echo -e "  ${RED}failed${NC}  ${pull_failed[*]}"
  fi
  echo ""
}

# ── Main ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
main() {
  setup_global_gitignore

  if [[ "${1:-}" == "stop" ]]; then
    stop_all
  fi

  local selected
  selected=$(select_services)

  if [[ -z "$selected" ]]; then
    warn "No services selected. Exiting."
    exit 0
  fi

  local -a chosen
  mapfile -t chosen <<< "$selected"

  header "Selected: ${chosen[*]}"

  # ── Mode: code-only (pull + Claude) or full (servers + browser + Claude)
  echo ""
  printf "  ${CYAN}1${NC}) code only  (pull + Claude)\n"
  printf "  ${CYAN}2${NC}) full       (pull + servers + browser + Claude)\n"
  echo ""
  read -rp "Mode [2]: " mode </dev/tty
  mode=${mode:-2}

  if [[ "$mode" == "1" ]]; then
    # Code-only: sweep + pull, then launch Claude in the first selected repo
    sweep_and_pull chosen

    # cd into the first selected repo and launch Claude
    entry=$(get_service "${chosen[0]}")
    IFS='|' read -r name cwd _ _ _ _ <<< "$entry"
    cd "$cwd"
    header "Launching Claude Code ($CLAUDE_MODEL) in $name..."
    exec claude --model "$CLAUDE_MODEL" --dangerously-skip-permissions
  fi

  # ── Step 1: Auto-sweep + pull ────────────────────────────────────────
  sweep_and_pull chosen

  # ── Step 3: Start backends via pm2 ─────────────────────────────────
  header "Starting backends via pm2..."
  # Only stop services we're about to (re)start — leave others running
  for svc in "${chosen[@]}"; do
    $PM2 delete "$svc-backend" 2>/dev/null || true
    $PM2 delete "$svc-frontend" 2>/dev/null || true
  done

  for svc in "${chosen[@]}"; do
    entry=$(get_service "$svc")
    IFS='|' read -r name cwd cmd port fe_dir fe_port <<< "$entry"
    free_port "$port"
    info "Starting $name backend on port $port"
    $PM2 start bash --name "$name-backend" --cwd "$cwd" --no-autorestart -- -c "$cmd"
  done

  $PM2 save 2>/dev/null || true

  # ── Step 4: Start Vite frontends with explicit ports ────────────────
  header "Starting Vite frontends..."
  local frontend_started=0
  for svc in "${chosen[@]}"; do
    entry=$(get_service "$svc")
    IFS='|' read -r name cwd _ _ fe_dir fe_port <<< "$entry"
    [[ -z "$fe_dir" ]] && continue

    # Resolve frontend working directory
    local fe_cwd="$cwd/$fe_dir"
    [[ "$fe_dir" == "." ]] && fe_cwd="$cwd"

    if [[ ! -f "$fe_cwd/package.json" ]]; then
      warn "$name: no package.json at $fe_cwd, skipping frontend"
      continue
    fi

    # Auto-install node_modules if missing
    if [[ ! -d "$fe_cwd/node_modules" ]]; then
      info "Installing $name frontend dependencies..."
      (cd "$fe_cwd" && npm install --legacy-peer-deps 2>&1 | tail -1)
    fi

    # Use npx vite with explicit --port to avoid collisions
    free_port "$fe_port"
    info "Starting $name frontend on port $fe_port"
    $PM2 start bash --name "$name-frontend" --cwd "$fe_cwd" -- -c "npx vite --host 0.0.0.0 --port $fe_port"
    frontend_started=1
  done

  if (( frontend_started == 0 )); then
    info "No Vite frontends to start for selected services."
  fi

  # ── Step 5: Wait for services to become ready ───────────────────────
  header "Waiting for services to become ready..."
  for svc in "${chosen[@]}"; do
    entry=$(get_service "$svc")
    IFS='|' read -r name _ _ port _ _ <<< "$entry"

    deadline=$((SECONDS + 45))
    ready=0
    while (( SECONDS < deadline )); do
      # DCL reports {"phase": "ready"} when fully warmed up.
      # Other services just need to respond to a health endpoint.
      if [[ "$name" == "dcl" ]]; then
        phase=$(curl -sf "http://localhost:$port/api/health" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('phase',''))" 2>/dev/null)
        if [[ "$phase" == "ready" || "$phase" == "degraded" ]]; then
          ok "$name backend ready (port $port, phase=$phase)"
          ready=1
          break
        elif [[ -n "$phase" ]]; then
          info "$name backend responding (phase=$phase), waiting for ready..."
        fi
      else
        if curl -sf "http://localhost:$port/health" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
           || curl -sf "http://localhost:$port/api/health" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
           || curl -sf "http://localhost:$port/api/v1/health" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          ok "$name backend ready (port $port)"
          ready=1
          break
        fi
      fi
      sleep 2
    done

    if (( ready == 0 )); then
      warn "$name backend not ready after 45s (port $port) — continuing"
    fi
  done

  # ── Step 6: Show status ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  $PM2 list

  # ── Step 7: Build preview URL list and open browsers ────────────────
  local -a preview_urls=()

  for svc in "${chosen[@]}"; do
    entry=$(get_service "$svc")
    IFS='|' read -r name _ _ port fe_dir fe_port <<< "$entry"
    if [[ -n "$fe_dir" && -n "$fe_port" ]]; then
      preview_urls+=("http://localhost:$fe_port")
    else
      preview_urls+=("http://localhost:$port")
    fi
  done

  for url in "${preview_urls[@]}"; do
    open_url "$url"
  done

  # ── Step 8: Print URL table (last thing visible before Claude) ─────
  echo ""
  header "Service URLs"
  echo ""
  printf "  ${BOLD}%-12s %-34s %s${NC}\n" "SERVICE" "BACKEND" "FRONTEND"
  printf "  %-12s %-34s %s\n" "───────────" "──────────────────────────────────" "──────────────────────────────────"

  for svc in "${chosen[@]}"; do
    entry=$(get_service "$svc")
    IFS='|' read -r name _ _ port fe_dir fe_port <<< "$entry"
    local be_url="http://localhost:$port"
    if [[ -n "$fe_dir" && -n "$fe_port" ]]; then
      printf "  %-12s %-34s %s\n" "$name" "$be_url" "http://localhost:$fe_port"
    else
      printf "  %-12s %-34s %s\n" "$name" "$be_url" "—"
    fi
  done

  echo ""
  ok "All selected services are running. Browser tabs opened."
  echo ""
  echo -e "  ${BOLD}aos stop${NC}       shut everything down"
  echo -e "  ${BOLD}npx pm2 logs${NC}   tail all logs"
  echo -e "  ${BOLD}npx pm2 monit${NC}  dashboard"
  echo ""
  header "Launching Claude Code ($CLAUDE_MODEL)..."
  exec claude --model "$CLAUDE_MODEL" --dangerously-skip-permissions
}

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# aos-sweep.sh — Exit scrub for all AOS repos
# Stages dirty trees, commits as WIP, pushes to remote.
# Run this before walking away or switching machines.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

# ── Ensure git identity is set (prevents interactive prompt mid-sweep) ──
if ! git config --global user.name >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    git config --global user.name "Ilya Cantor"
fi
if ! git config --global user.email >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    git config --global user.email "ilyac@example.com"
fi

TIMESTAMP=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
SHORT_TS=$(date '+%Y%m%d-%H%M')

content.text:
Done. Both scripts now ensure `user.name` and `user.email` are set globally before any git operations:

- **`aos-sweep.sh`** — identity guard added at lines 8-14
- **`aos-launch.sh`** — identity guard added at lines 4-6

The checks are idempotent — they only write if the config isn't already set, so once you've run either script once, the global config persists and the guard becomes a no-op.

message.content: on each session does it re-require?

content.text: No. `git config --global` writes to `~/.gitconfig`, which persists on disk permanently. After the first run sets it, every future session already has it — the guards just become silent no-ops.
